title: Blechnum pygmaeum
intro: Blechnum pygmaeum, also known as the dwarf chain fern, is a type of fern that belongs to the Blechnaceae family. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can aid in reducing inflammation and protecting against oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Blechnum pygmaeum include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Blechnum pygmaeum include teas, infusions, and tinctures, often used to support overall health and well-being.
taxonomy: Blechnum pygmaeum is a species of fern in the family Blechnaceae, within the order Polypodiales. It is native to New Zealand. Blechnum pygmaeum is part of the genus Blechnum, which is a group of small to medium-sized terrestrial ferns. The family Blechnaceae is a group of ferns commonly known as chain ferns or rock ferns. Blechnum pygmaeum is classified as an endangered species due to habitat loss and degradation.
